_comment: Canned, obviously-synthetic model responses for the council quickstart. No real API is called. Each critic value is a list of lines joined with newlines by demo_council.py; the fenced JSON block conforms to critic.schema.json. The spec under review is a toy 'Acme Widget API v1'.

1: Write endpoints specify no authentication. The Endpoints section lists POST/PATCH/DELETE /widgets as open, so any caller can mutate widget state. Require a bearer token on every write path.

{"seat":"seat_1","path":"parallel","outcome":"COMPLETED","findings":[{"severity":"BLOCKER","verdict":"ACCEPT","claim":"Write endpoints specify no authentication and are described as open.","rationale":"The Endpoints section lists POST/PATCH/DELETE /widgets with no auth requirement, so any caller can mutate widget state.","patch_direction":"Require a bearer token on every write path and define the 401 response.","spec_location":"Endpoints"}]}
2: The list endpoint GET /widgets defines no pagination or result cap. A large widget table returns unbounded payloads. Add limit and cursor query params with a documented maximum page size.

{"seat":"seat_2","path":"parallel","outcome":"COMPLETED","findings":[{"severity":"REAL","verdict":"ACCEPT","claim":"The list endpoint GET /widgets defines no pagination or result cap.","rationale":"With no limit or cursor, a large widget table returns unbounded payloads and can exhaust memory on both server and client.","patch_direction":"Add limit plus cursor query params and a documented default and maximum page size.","spec_location":"Endpoints"}]}
3: No error response schema is defined for any endpoint. Clients cannot distinguish validation errors from server faults. Define a single error envelope and list which codes each endpoint returns.

{"seat":"seat_3","path":"parallel","outcome":"COMPLETED","findings":[{"severity":"REAL","verdict":"DECIDE","claim":"No error response schema is defined for any endpoint.","rationale":"The spec shows only success bodies, so clients cannot reliably distinguish validation errors from server faults; the shape needs a product decision.","patch_direction":"Define a single error envelope with a code and message field and list which codes each endpoint returns.","spec_location":"Responses"}]}
4: No rate limiting or abuse control is mentioned for the public API. An open write API with no throttling invites floods, though the right limit depends on expected traffic.

{"seat":"seat_4","path":"parallel","outcome":"COMPLETED","findings":[{"severity":"SPECULATIVE","verdict":"DECIDE","claim":"No rate limiting or abuse control is mentioned for the public API.","rationale":"An open write API with no throttling invites accidental or hostile floods, though the right limit depends on expected traffic volume.","patch_direction":"Decide a default per-token rate limit and document the 429 response and retry-after header.","spec_location":"Non-functional"}]}
5: The delete endpoint performs a hard delete with no authorization check. Like the other write paths it requires no caller identity, compounding the auth gap. Gate deletes behind auth.

{"seat":"seat_5","path":"parallel","outcome":"COMPLETED","findings":[{"severity":"BLOCKER","verdict":"ACCEPT","claim":"The delete endpoint performs a hard delete with no authorization check.","rationale":"DELETE /widgets/{id} removes rows permanently and, like the other write paths, requires no caller identity, compounding the auth gap.","patch_direction":"Gate deletes behind auth and consider a soft-delete or confirmation step.","spec_location":"Endpoints"}]}

judge_parallel: 1. [seat_1, seat_5] [BLOCKER] [ACCEPT] Write paths (including hard delete) require no authentication. Two independent critics; high confidence.
2. [seat_2] [REAL] [ACCEPT] GET /widgets has no pagination or result cap.
3. [seat_3] [REAL] [DECIDE] No error response schema is defined.
4. [seat_4] [SPECULATIVE] [DECIDE] No rate limiting on the public API.
APPLY: findings 1 and 2 (auth on all write paths; pagination on list).
DECIDE: findings 3 and 4 (error envelope shape; rate-limit policy) need a product call.
None material. Severity differs on abuse control (SPECULATIVE) vs auth (BLOCKER), which is expected.
judge_sequential: 2. [seat_2] [REAL] [ACCEPT] GET /widgets has no pagination; builds on finding 1 (an open, unbounded list is worse).
3. [seat_3] [REAL] [DECIDE] No error response schema; the chain noted this blocks client handling of the auth failures above.
None. The chain reinforced rather than contradicted the parallel reads.
reviewer: - Coverage 8/10: auth, pagination, errors, and abuse control all surfaced.
- Independence 9/10: path-A and path-B reached the auth finding without shared context.
- Signal-to-noise 8/10: one SPECULATIVE finding, correctly marked.
- Judge quality per path 8/10: both judges grouped equivalent findings cleanly.
- Abstention handling 10/10: no seat abstained; none was dropped.
- Substantive vs cosmetic 9/10: the top findings are real security and scaling gaps.
- New problems introduced 0: no finding contradicts a spec requirement that exists.
- Systemic blind spots: neither path checked auth on the read paths; minor.
- Patterns different? Path-B chained findings; path-A found more breadth (rate limiting).
- Both findings equivalent? Yes: the auth finding is genuinely the same defect.
